message.content:
POLARIS MISSION (this wheel): Make the agentic outline top-benchmark: it must COMPUTE-and-PROVE numbers (the moat frontier products lack), process the FULL 346-basket corpus with cp4_used=agentic (never fallback-plain; seed-fallback only on GLM-5.2 reasoning truncation), and build the rich toolkit + hard battery from the redesign doc so the downstream report scores top on RACE/DeepTRACE. Read /workspace/POLARIS/docs/agentic_outline_redesign.md (read-only) for the binding T1/T2 spec.
SUCCESS IS MEASURED BY: (a) A computed number (e.g. a delta/ratio) can be COMPUTED by the agent AND RENDER in the outline via the verified [#calc:] lane (quantified_analysis.py:617), never via [CITE:ev_xxx] — proven end-to-end on one real case; (b) the T1 toolkit tools land and pass real unit tests; (c) the T2 battery runs and S0/S1 failures trend to zero. Faithfulness invariant is the hard gate: NO derived/unverified number may ever reach the [CITE:ev_xxx] render path (that would be a P0 breach).
The bar is not "bug fixed" — it is the benchmark number at the top, VERIFIED. Keep going until the
measurement says top (or you hit a hard external blocker you must report honestly).

FENCE (military discipline — no exceptions):
- Work ONLY inside /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent (branch bot/outline-agent-box). NEVER touch another worktree.
- NEVER edit anything under /home/polaris/polaris_project (operator's monitoring dir — deck.sh, wheel*.js, wheels.py).
  If you catch yourself editing a monitoring script or a workflow .js, you have DRIFTED — stop.
- Report REAL output only. Never invent, never claim a pass you did not observe, never fake a benchmark number.
- Commit every landed step in /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent; append one dated line to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md.


YOU ARE FABLE — the scalpel. You are NOT here to read or search the codebase; Opus already did the
hard reading. You are here for ONE precise deep-think: judge what Opus did and decide the next step.

WHAT OPUS DID THIS ROUND:
{
  "did": [
    "Reproduced the 7 inherited reds, then root-caused them as THREE distinct divergences (Fable's all-7=missing-kwarg diagnosis was incomplete): (1) 1 genuine stale double missing the reasoning_max_tokens kwarg; (2) 5 tests pinning the legacy 150/40 row-cap that P0-A20 dissolved by flipping PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN default-ON (overrides the fixture's existing PG_GEN_ROW_CAPS=1); (3) 1 wording-drift assertion after inherited 2b42bc5 reworded 'Mechanism must be ADDITIVE'->'Mechanism is ADDITIVE'.",
    "Fixed all three test-only: added reasoning_max_tokens=None to 3 fake generate() doubles; added PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN=0 to the iperm011 legacy-path fixture (faithful to its documented byte-identical-legacy intent); updated the clinical-retry assertion to the live string after PROVING via a live _call_outline(domain='clinical') that 'Mechanism is ADDITIVE' AND 'Efficacy' are still present (semantics intact).",
    "Resolved the 346-basket corpus to an honest discrepancy: measured every persisted cp3 dump. Canonical consolidated corpus = 328-329 TOTAL baskets (cp3c_dump=328; s3_gear consolidation_summary.basket_total=329, two independent dumps agree), NOT 346. '346' appears in no doc/manifest. Only s3_hamster_i1 (581) exceeds 346 but is a less-consolidated different run (51 same_work_groups, 69 multi-member) — using it would be inflating.",
    "Identified two hard pre-flight blockers for the staged sweep command as written: (i) DEFINITIONAL — sweep _count_baskets counts only multi-member clusters (member_indices>=2), maxing at 38/53/69 across all dumps, so --min-baskets 346 is unsatisfiable under the sweep's own counter regardless of corpus; (ii) SCHEMA — no persisted dump matches the sweep runtime format (research_question/evidence/finding_clusters[member_indices]); the cp3 snapshot uses question/payload.baskets[member_evidence_ids] and carries NO evidence pool, so --corpus <snapshot> cannot even load without a converter + the cp2 evidence pool.",
    "Snapshotted both canonical corpora on-disk into the worktree (durable vs volatile /tmp): data/cp3_s3gear_329basket_snapshot.json (329) + data/cp3c_dump_328basket.json (328); recorded concrete paths + full escalation in WHEEL_PROGRESS.md. (data/ is gitignored so committed the progress log + test fixes only; snapshots persist on-disk.)"
  ],
  "commits": [
    "9e8b58d"
  ],
  "files": [
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/tests/polaris_graph/test_iperm011_outline_evidence_cap.py",
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/tests/polaris_graph/test_generator_domain_outline_iready009.py",
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md",
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/data/cp3_s3gear_329basket_snapshot.json",
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/data/cp3c_dump_328basket.json"
  ],
  "exercised": "\"Ran the two fixed files (34/34), the combined tracked+hygiene suite (170/170), and the T2 battery (--all, 11/11 S0..S4=0) — real pytest runs, not py_compile. Proved the clinical-retry wording fix by DRIVING _call_outline(domain='clinical') end-to-end and asserting the live captured retry prompt contains 'Mechanism is ADDITIVE'=True and 'Efficacy'=True. Proved the corpus counts by loading and counting every persisted cp3 dump (json). Verified schema gap by direct field inspection (snapshot has 'question' not 'research_question', no 'evidence', no 'finding_clusters'/'member_indices').\"",
  "measurement": "\"Test-double hygiene: the two target files go 34/34 (was 7 red). Combined committed check: 170/170 green (34 hygiene + 136 tracked outline/digest/checkpoint/revise suite — tracked 136/136 UNCHANGED). T2 battery scripts/outline_battery.py --all = 11/11 PASS S0=S1=S2=S3=S4=0 (run 20260711T213745Z) including h01a_calc_render (metric (a) offline [#calc:] render proof preserved). Corpus measurement: canonical consolidated full cp3 corpus = 328-329 TOTAL baskets (not 346); sweep _count_baskets multi-member count maxes at 69 across all dumps. Live 346-basket cp4_used=agentic RACE/DeepTRACE number NOT measurable: os.getenv('OPENROUTER_API_KEY')=False re-verified twice this round.\"",
  "open": [
    "The live full-corpus cp4_used=agentic RACE/DeepTRACE number (the sole gap to top) — credential-gated.",
    "A CP3->CP4 corpus converter (rename question->research_question, rebuild finding_clusters/member_indices, re-attach cp2 evidence pool) does not yet exist; without it the sweep cannot load any persisted dump even when the key lands.",
    "The sweep --min-baskets target/definition mismatch (346 vs canonical 329 total; multi-member vs total-basket counter) is unresolved pending operator confirmation."
  ],
  "status": "BLOCKED",
  "blockers": [
    "OPENROUTER_API_KEY absent (os.getenv->False, re-verified twice) — the live GLM-5.2 decide loop cannot run one turn, so the 346(->329)-basket cp4_used=agentic RACE/DeepTRACE number is hard-blocked. External credential only.",
    "Staged sweep command `--min-baskets 346` is doubly unsatisfiable even with a key: (a) the sweep counter measures multi-member clusters (<=69), not total baskets; (b) no persisted dump matches the sweep runtime schema — a CP3->CP4 converter + cp2 evidence pool is required to materialize a loadable corpus. Needs an operator/Fable decision on target + definition before the sweep can fire."
  ]
}

OPUS'S PRECISE QUESTION FOR YOU:
"Given the canonical consolidated corpus is 328-329 TOTAL baskets (not 346), that sweep _count_baskets measures only multi-member clusters (<=69, so --min-baskets 346 can never pass), and that NO persisted dump matches the sweep's runtime schema (needs a converter + the cp2 evidence pool): is closing this gap myself the sanctioned next offline pre-flight — i.e. build an offline CP3->CP4 converter that materializes a sweep-loadable corpus from data/cp3_s3gear_329basket_snapshot.json + the cp2 evidence pool and retune --min-baskets to the total-basket count (~300-329) — OR must I HOLD for operator confirmation of the intended basket target and definition before touching the sweep, since changing the counter/target is arguably an operator decision not a unilateral fix?"

PRIOR ROUNDS (for continuity):
[
  {
    "r": 1,
    "meas": "Outline test suite went 19/19 -> 46/46 green (~2.0s). Metric (a) PROVEN with real numbers: Adobe FY2015->FY2016 operating-income delta $590,507,000.00 (real FinanceBench figures 1,493,602 - 903,095 thousands, *1000) COMPUTES via build_quantified_spec->execute_quantified_model and RENDERS via [#calc:opinc_delta:HASH:delta] -> strict_verify CALC LANE kept=1 dropped=0; the SAME derived number via [#ev:] -> kept=0 dropped=1 (number_not_in_any_cited_span). Metric (b): 7 T1 tools land + 21 real unit tests pass; verified_compute drives through the real _execute dispatch (rendered $1,493,602,000.00 via [#calc:m:HASH:v], model registered). Metric (c) T2 battery: NOT started this round (deferred), so S0/S1 not yet measurable.",
    "verdict": "CONTINUE",
    "next": "Build the T2 battery (scripts/outline_battery.py + cases/fixtures per the redesign doc) as the measurement instrument for metric (c), and include: (i) S0 calc-lane cases including a wrong-pair-subtraction semantic case (DEFER the mirror-model question<->formula entailment check — fail-open LLM checks cannot strengthen a fail-closed gate; add it only if this battery case shows the mode fires); (ii) one integration case that drives an outline-emitted [#calc:] sentence + workspace.quantified_models through the DOWNSTREAM strict_verify handoff — this will expose and force the fix that multi_section_generator.py:7955 and honest_pipeline.py:336 never receive the registry (plumb workspace.quantified_models into those calls so the computed number actually ships in production, not just in the probe); (iii) H07/H22 contradiction cases marked xfail until find_contradictions lands. Build fetch_url immediately after (trivial now via fold_in_fetched_rows at _fold_in.py:150), then find_contradictions/corroboration_profile to flip H07/H22 from xfail to measured."
  },
  {
    "r": 2,
    "meas": "T2 battery (run 20260711T200013Z, git a135789): 7/7 PASS, S0=0 S1=0 S2=0 S3=0 S4=0 (obtained via `python scripts/outline_battery.py --all`, recorded in outputs/battery/history.jsonl). Production handoff proven through the REAL run_honest_pipeline: legacy (quantified_models=None) => sentences_verified=0 and $590,507,000.00 ABSENT from final_report_text; with the registry => sentences_verified=1 and the number PRESENT in the production report. Outline unit suite: 54/54 green (~2s). Toolkit now 9 tools, each with real unit tests.",
    "verdict": "CONTINUE",
    "next": "Wire the registry across the live seam: (1) export workspace.quantified_models from run_outline_agent_or_legacy (outline_agent.py:2040) — cleanest is an extra attribute on parse_result or a widened return consumed at multi_section_generator.py:9669; (2) thread it (default None => byte-identical) into the section-body verify call sites — the verify_sentence_provenance calls at multi_section_generator.py:4437, 4701, 5442, 5886 and the strict_verify tail — so a [#calc:] body sentence verifies against the outline agent's computed models; (3) give the compose/writer path the verified claim sentences (verified_compute's claim text) as candidate body sentences so the token can actually be emitted; then prove it with a battery case that drives generate_multi_section_report (not run_honest_pipeline) from outline-agent workspace to number-in-report, and only after that attempt the full-corpus cp4_used=agentic run."
  },
  {
    "r": 3,
    "meas": "Metric (a) PROVEN on the full-corpus composer path (deterministic, offline): _run_section with the agentic registry renders \\\"Adobe's operating income rose by $590,507,000.00.[1][2]\\\" (sentences_verified=1, dropped=0; [1][2] resolve to the two 10-K input spans ev_2016/ev_2015); WITHOUT the registry the SAME body sentence is dropped and the section renders its gap stub (verified=0, number absent) — observed via a direct print of result.verified_text both ways. Metric (c): T2 battery 8/8 PASS, S0=S1=S2=S3=S4=0 (added h01f_composer_handoff). Outline suite 56/56 green. Regression: generator batch identical to HEAD baseline (11 pre-existing pollution failures, +0 from this diff, verified by HEAD swap). Faithfulness invariant intact: h01b drop-guard + calc_mixed_with_ev_token gate untouched, so derived numbers still cannot reach [#ev:]/[CITE:].",
    "verdict": "CONTINUE",
    "next": "Build the deterministic EMISSION channel (round-2 item 3, still open): export the render-ready claim sentences alongside the registry at the seam (outline_agent.py:2042 — e.g. parse_result.calc_claims: section_title -> [claim.render_sentence(...)], sourced from the agent's verified_compute results in final_ws), capture them next to _outline_quantified_models in generate_multi_section_report (:9705), and inject them DETERMINISTICALLY into the section's rewritten text immediately before the strict_verify call in _run_section (multi_section_generator.py:6160) — deterministic append, not prompt instruction, because an LLM cannot be trusted to copy a hash verbatim, and fail-closed safety is free: the appended sentence only survives if the registry verifies it (drop-guard h01b already proves the [#ev:] path stays sealed). Then re-prove the differential with a _call_section stub that does NOT pre-contain the token, add a battery case for it, and only then is the remaining gap purely credentials."
  },
  {
    "r": 4,
    "meas": "Metric (a) CLOSED on the live-shaped composer path: battery h01g_emission_channel PASS — with a token-free writer draft, the derived $590,507,000.00 RENDERS in _run_section.verified_text via [#calc:opinc_delta:HASH:delta] only when the emission channel is ON (sentences_verified>=1), is ABSENT when OFF, and is DROPPED when the backing registry is absent. Metric (b): outline suite 63/63 green (was 56; +7 tests). Metric (c): T2 battery 9/9 PASS, S0=0 S1=0 S2=0 S3=0 S4=0 (was 8/8). Faithfulness gate intact (h01b + h01g-F drop guards). Numbers obtained by real runs: `pytest tests/polaris_graph/outline/` and `python scripts/outline_battery.py --all` (run 20260711T203201Z).",
    "verdict": "CONTINUE",
    "next": "Append-standalone is ruled acceptable, so no second emission CODE task exists — spend the round closing the last open T1 item and making the wheel key-drop-ready: (1) build corroboration_profile per the binding spec (docs/agentic_outline_redesign.md:107 — per-basket distinct works, tier/weight spread; note redesign.md:217's rule that single-work corroboration silently counted as multi-work = FAIL, so surface distinct-WORK counts, not member counts) as a new tool in src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_toolkit.py following the inspect_basket pattern (:204), with real unit tests + a battery case for the single-work-masquerade failure; (2) do the deferred decide-prompt CORE/index scaling in outline_agent.py; (3) stage a one-command 346-basket cp4_used=agentic sweep script (asserting cp4_used==agentic per basket, seed-fallback only on GLM-5.2 truncation) so metric (a)-full-corpus fires the instant credentials land; (4) escalate the credentials blocker explicitly in WHEEL_PROGRESS.md as the sole remaining gate — do not fake or proxy the RACE/DeepTRACE number."
  },
  {
    "r": 5,
    "meas": "Offline everything green, reproduced this round: outline suite 107/107 (was 63); T2 battery 11/11 PASS S0=S1=S2=S3=S4=0 (run 20260711T205344Z, added h_corr1/h_corr2); ReAct 228/228 + tools 5/5; sweep --dry-run PASSED (exit 0, all 5 cp4 cases + basket counter correct). corroboration_profile PROVEN on a real masquerade: 3 same-DOI-different-URL rows -> distinct_works=1, single_work_masquerade=True, and the cp3 digest's url-first count (3) flagged as a silent overcount. metric (a)-FULL-CORPUS + RACE/DeepTRACE: NOT measurable — verified OPENROUTER_API_KEY absent (only CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ACCESS_TOKEN in env); the agentic outliner calls GLM-5.2 via that key (openrouter_client.py:58), so the live sweep cannot run. No proxy/fake number produced.",
    "verdict": "CONTINUE",
    "next": "RULING on the layering question: (b) is correct and the premise of (a) is wrong — the '[xN works]' head renders ONLY into the planner prompt (multi_section_generator.py:3077-3086, explicitly 'not a faithfulness gate'); shipped-report corroboration stats use verified_support_origin_count (multi_section_generator.py:126-222), so there is no DeepTRACE render liability and NO change to finding_dedup._same_work_key precedence (deliberate STEP-4 design, kill-switched, wide blast radius). BUT the over-count is a real planner-quality bias: outline_digest.py:533 sorts baskets by -work_count, so a same-DOI-different-URL pseudo-corroborated basket outranks genuine multi-work baskets. Next action: land a contained DOI fold in _build_alias_map (outline_digest.py:~244), mirroring the existing item-2 title fold — group rows by finding_dedup._normalize_doi and adopt an overlapping cp3 group's key so same-DOI-different-URL copies unify into one work; byte-identical when no same-DOI split exists; add a digest test asserting basket_work_corroboration==1 on the 3-row same-DOI-different-URL fixture and that corroboration_profile's digest_disagreement tripwire then reads False. While there, close the deferred calc_claims duplicate-section-title uniqueness guard. Keep the OPENROUTER_API_KEY escalation standing as the sole external gate; never proxy the live number."
  },
  {
    "r": 6,
    "meas": "outline+digest suite 111/111 green (was 107; +4 new tests all pass). T2 battery re-run (scripts/outline_battery.py --all): 11/11 PASS, severity failures S0=0 S1=0 S2=0 S3=0 S4=0. End-to-end DOI-fold measurement obtained by actually running build_outline_digest on a 3-row same-DOI-different-URL fixture: basket_work_corroboration['B00']==1 (was 3 pre-fix), and feeding that real menu into the real _tool_corroboration_profile yields digest_disagreement=False. calc_claims guard discrimination measured directly: OLD list logic homes to 'Costs', NEW union to 'Efficacy'. Live 346-basket cp4_used=agentic RACE/DeepTRACE number NOT measurable: OPENROUTER_API_KEY verified absent again this round (os.getenv -> False), so GLM-5.2 decide loop cannot run a single turn.",
    "verdict": "CONTINUE",
    "next": "RULING: keep the len(stamped)>=2 refusal at outline_digest.py:297 — it is the correct conservative choice, and the worry-case premise is empirically false: the real cp3 payload (/workspace/POLARIS/outputs/s3_gear/cp3_basket_snapshot.json) contains NO singleton groups (all 55 groups have >=2 members), so a one-row-per-URL same-DOI pair arrives UNCLAIMED and the fold already merges it (the measured 3->1 case); the refusal only bites when two MULTI-member url: groups share a DOI, which has zero incidence in the real corpus (5/654 rows have usable DOIs; 0 DOIs span >=2 cp3 groups), and a member-level override would SPLIT a cp3 group (non-DOI siblings left on the old key) unless key-level union-find remapping were built — unjustified for zero incidence and it would make the digest override cp3's deliberate url-first STEP-4 identity. Next action (one small commit): lock the ruling with (1) a test in tests/polaris_graph/test_outline_digest.py encoding the two-multi-member-url-groups-shared-DOI case and asserting NO merge (stamped members keep their url: keys, unclaimed members fold to doi:), and (2) a cheap observability tripwire — count DOI groups hitting the len(stamped)>=2 branch and surface it (e.g. in corroboration_profile or a digest stat + a per-basket log line in scripts/outline_agentic_sweep.py) so if the zero-incidence assumption breaks on the 346-basket corpus it is visible, not silent. Then the wheel is code-complete: keep the OPENROUTER_API_KEY escalation standing in WHEEL_PROGRESS.md as the sole gate and run the staged sweep the moment the key lands — never proxy the live number."
  },
  {
    "r": 7,
    "meas": "REAL numbers this round: (1) outline+digest suite 112/112 green (was 111, +1 new test); T2 battery `outline_battery.py --all` 11/11 PASS with S0=S1=S2=S3=S4=0; sweep --dry-run PASSED. (2) Ruling-validation measurement on the real cp3 snapshot (55 same_work_groups, 385 reconstructed member rows, 13 with usable 10. DOIs parsed from member_urls): DOIs spanning >=2 cp3 groups = 0; measured false-merge guard hits = 0 (stats dict empty). The refusal never bites on the real corpus. (3) The mission's live 346-basket cp4_used=agentic RACE/DeepTRACE number remains UNMEASURABLE: OPENROUTER_API_KEY absent (re-verified os.getenv -> False), so the GLM-5.2 decide loop cannot run a single turn — hard external blocker, no proxy produced.",
    "verdict": "CONTINUE",
    "next": "HOLD at BLOCKED — land no further code. Each round: (1) re-check os.getenv('OPENROUTER_API_KEY'); (2) the instant it is set, run the staged command exactly: PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1 python scripts/outline_agentic_sweep.py --corpus <346-basket cp3 dump> --min-baskets 346, and record per-basket cp4_used==agentic, the false_merge_guard_hits doi=0 title=0 line (scripts/outline_agentic_sweep.py:238), and the RACE/DeepTRACE score in WHEEL_PROGRESS.md; (3) keep the credential escalation as the sole standing item. If any guard hit is non-zero on the live corpus, that (and only that) reopens code work: the key-level union-find remap flagged in outline_digest.py:306."
  },
  {
    "r": 8,
    "meas": "OFFLINE at ceiling, LIVE still blocked. Outline+digest+toolkit suite 136/136 green; T2 battery 11/11 PASS with S0=S1=S2=S3=S4=0 (run 20260711T212346Z) including h01a_calc_render (verified [#calc:] render-lane proof for metric (a) on one real case); sweep --dry-run PASSED. The mission's live number (346-basket cp4_used=agentic RACE/DeepTRACE) is NOT measurable: OPENROUTER_API_KEY absent (os.getenv -> False, re-verified 3 ways); the agentic outliner calls GLM-5.2 via that key (openrouter_client.py:58), so not a single decide turn can execute. No proxy number produced.",
    "verdict": "CONTINUE",
    "next": "Two offline actions, both permitted under an amended HOLD (test-only/doc-only, zero production surface): (1) PRIMARY — resolve the 346-basket corpus before the key lands: locate a cp3 dump with >=346 baskets (search /workspace/POLARIS/outputs/ and the s3_gear snapshots; /tmp/cp3c_dump.json@328 rows does not satisfy --min-baskets 346), snapshot the chosen dump into the worktree (e.g. /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/data/) since /tmp is volatile, and record the concrete --corpus path in WHEEL_PROGRESS.md; if the real full corpus is genuinely 328 baskets, document that honestly and escalate the 346-vs-328 discrepancy to the operator rather than inflating. (2) HOLD-amended hygiene — add reasoning_max_tokens=None (or **kwargs) to _CapturingClient.generate at tests/polaris_graph/test_iperm011_outline_evidence_cap.py:73 and :256 and the sibling fake in test_generator_domain_outline_iready009.py, matching production multi_section_generator.py:3156; one test-only commit, then re-verify those files go 34/34 and the tracked 136/136 + battery 11/11 S0..S4=0 are unchanged. Keep the per-round key check; fire the staged sweep the instant OPENROUTER_API_KEY appears. Never proxy the live number."
  }
]

DECIDE, precisely:
1. at_top: does the REAL measurement show top benchmark performance? ((a) A computed number (e.g. a delta/ratio) can be COMPUTED by the agent AND RENDER in the outline via the verified [#calc:] lane (quantified_analysis.py:617), never via [CITE:ev_xxx] — proven end-to-end on one real case; (b) the T1 toolkit tools land and pass real unit tests; (c) the T2 battery runs and S0/S1 failures trend to zero. Faithfulness invariant is the hard gate: NO derived/unverified number may ever reach the [CITE:ev_xxx] render path (that would be a P0 breach).)
2. correct: is the diff correct for what it claims?
3. faithfulness_safe: are the invariants intact (no derived/unverified number can render; fold-in seam intact)?
4. verdict: SIGN_OFF only if at_top AND correct AND faithfulness_safe. Else CONTINUE (or FIX_REGRESSION if
   Opus broke something). NEVER force-approve. A measurement Opus could not take is NOT a sign-off.
5. next_step: the single most important next action for Opus — precise, file:line where you can.
Think deeply here; this is the one place the wheel spends deep reasoning. Keep it tight and decisive.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch
